name: John Carter
abstract: John Wallace Carter (September 24, 1929 – March 31, 1991) was an American jazz clarinet, saxophone, and flute player. He is noted for the acclaimed Roots and Folklore series, a five-album concept album set inspired by African American life and experiences.

about_quotes.speaker: Red Callender
about_quotes.evidence: John Carter, clarinet; I forgot to mention that. ... John Carter played clarinet; ... That covers all the musicians.

about_quotes.speaker: Hayes Pillars
about_quotes.evidence: We had a boy named John Carter on the piano. In that band, we had John Carter on the piano

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: The last one was John Carter, who was my manager at the time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: David Murray
about_quotes.evidence: I suggested that he maybe should interview Bobby Bradford and John Carter. But John Carter was already teaching... So Bobby came in.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Murray interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 18, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Hutcherson
about_quotes.evidence: Still no, not really. I didn’t know those people until I got back.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Hutcherson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
